title: Update architecture.md and tech-decisions.md to record Material UI as the frontend component library

conversation_log.summary: Task is architecturally sound. Documentation-only changes that correctly precede implementation. Approved with notes: (1) The new ADR must be numbered ADR-008 following the existing sequence in tech-decisions.md. (2) The components/ directory addition in the Structure section should include a brief responsibility note consistent with the existing 'Directory Responsibilities' subsection. (3) The MUI pattern added to Key Patterns should be numbered sequentially (Pattern #9) following the existing convention.

All acceptance criteria are testable as written. Each criterion maps to a specific, observable, binary-verifiable change in one of two documentation files: (1) ADR-008 present in tech-decisions.md with specified content (sole UI library, ThemeProvider/CssBaseline wrappers, sx prop as primary styling, MUI for layout/nav/typography). (2) Tech Stack table row in architecture.md with exact text 'Frontend component library | Material UI | ThemeProvider + CssBaseline at app root; sx prop for styling'. (3) Key Pattern #9 in architecture.md forbidding raw CSS/inline styles where sx/theme applies. (4) components/ directory entry in the Structure section of architecture.md. (5) Directory Responsibilities entry for components/ in architecture.md. (6) No application code changes — verifiable by confirming only docs/ files are modified.

Task executed by Architect. Changes made: (1) Added ADR-008 to docs/tech-decisions.md — records Material UI as the sole frontend component library with Emotion styling engine, ThemeProvider + CssBaseline wrappers, sx prop as primary styling, theme config in apps/frontend/src/lib/theme.ts. (2) Updated docs/architecture.md — added 'Frontend component library | Material UI' row to Tech Stack table, added components/layout/ directory and theme.ts to Structure section, added Directory Responsibilities entries for components/ and components/layout/, added Key Pattern #9 (Material UI as the Sole Styling and Component System).
